ctx.name: Tom
ctx.notes: Tom moved teams recently and delivery is steady, but Principal-level impact is not visible yet. Previous feedback said he needs early expectation alignment, and he still asks very few questions. I want to test whether he understands what Principal scope means on this team or if he is still operating on old assumptions. I also need to understand whether cross-team trust and judgment calls are developing or whether he is staying in safe execution mode.

focusPoints.label: Principal-level impact and leverage visibility
focusPoints.reason: What Principal-level impact should look like on this team, and whether his current work is showing up in that way yet.

focusPoints.label: What “Principal scope” means on this team
focusPoints.reason: Whether he understands the Principal bar on this team after moving teams, or if he’s still operating on old assumptions about scope and expectations.

focusPoints.label: Cross-team trust and expectation management
focusPoints.reason: Whether cross-team trust is developing—especially around setting expectations early and handling hard news—versus staying in safe execution mode.

focusPoints.label: Judgment calls: when to push vs escalate
focusPoints.reason: Whether he’s making and socializing the tradeoff calls that only a Principal should own, or if he’s avoiding questions and escalation when uncertainty is high.

transcript.question: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
transcript.answer: I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.

transcript.question: You said 'highest-leverage work' — what work specifically are you pointing at?
transcript.answer: I can describe Principal impact in general, but this team's bar still feels fuzzy.

transcript.question: This team's bar feels fuzzy — what specifically is still unclear to you?
transcript.answer: I have mostly stayed in my lane instead of forcing cross-team decisions.

transcript.question: What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?
transcript.answer: I have made a few calls independently, but I still over-index on being safe.

transcript.question: You said 'safe' — where did you hold back when you could have pushed harder?
transcript.answer: I probably should ask for sharper feedback earlier than I do.

transcript.question: Sharper feedback earlier — what stops you asking for it sooner?
transcript.answer: I have not built enough trust with adjacent teams yet.

transcript.question: Adjacent teams — where is the trust not there yet?
transcript.answer: The gap is less technical and more around visible ownership.

transcript.question: Where are you making the tradeoff call yourself instead of pushing it up?
transcript.answer: I need explicit expectations for the next cycle so I can calibrate fast.

transcript.question: What is the first concrete change you want to make before our next review?
